candidates.scope_justification: Four of five most-cited works in the manuscript appeared here (BBC97, FBA99, BBP97, BB01). Stated remit covers original computations in number theory, including precision-controlled experimental work; this is the literature-centroid match.
candidates.typical_paper_type_match: Yes — routinely publishes computational integer-relation, constants, and CF work. Null results appear when methodologically rigorous (e.g. precision bounds, certified search domains).
candidates.decision_time_estimate: [unverified, to verify Stage 3] AMS journals typically 6–12 months first decision
candidates.ai_disclosure_stance: [unverified, to verify Stage 3] AMS journals generally require author disclosure of AI assistance per AMS author guidelines; not a ban
candidates.oa_apc: [unverified, to verify Stage 3] AMS Open Math Notes / Hybrid OA option available; default subscription; APC not mandatory
candidates.editorial_signal: [unverified, to verify Stage 3] check current editorial board for editors with PSLQ/Khinchin/experimental-number-theory background
candidates.pseudonymity_friction: Low-to-moderate — AMS does not require institutional affiliation; ORCID accepted

candidates.venue: INTEGERS — Electronic Journal of Combinatorial Number Theory
candidates.scope_justification: Publishes short notes, including negative/null results in computational number theory. Khinchin-statistic + PSLQ work fits.
candidates.typical_paper_type_match: Yes — INTEGERS frequently publishes short certified-computation notes and null/negative results; format friendly to 6–8 page submissions.
candidates.decision_time_estimate: [unverified, to verify Stage 3] often 3–9 months for short notes
candidates.ai_disclosure_stance: [unverified, to verify Stage 3] no known AI ban; check editorial statement
candidates.editorial_signal: [unverified, to verify Stage 3] managing editor Bruce Landman; check board for Khinchin/CF editors
candidates.pseudonymity_friction: Low — diamond OA, ORCID accepted, no institutional requirement

candidates.scope_justification: Continued fractions, Khinchin-class statistics, and Bailey/Borwein-style computational results are recurrent topics. Khinchin-PSLQ is squarely on-topic.
candidates.typical_paper_type_match: Yes for CF work, but null-result tolerance unclear — needs Stage 3 TOC scan.
candidates.decision_time_estimate: [unverified, to verify Stage 3] Springer median 4–9 months
candidates.ai_disclosure_stance: [unverified, to verify Stage 3] Springer Nature policy: AI cannot be a co-author; tools must be disclosed in methods — permissive
candidates.oa_apc: Hybrid OA available (Springer Compact); high APC ~$3000 if chosen, but subscription default has no APC
candidates.editorial_signal: [unverified, to verify Stage 3] Krishnaswami Alladi as EIC (historical); check for CF/Khinchin editors

candidates.scope_justification: Broad number-theory venue with a computational-research stream. Khinchin/PSLQ falls under metric and computational number theory.
candidates.typical_paper_type_match: Mostly positive results; null-result tolerance moderate — needs Stage 3 check.
candidates.decision_time_estimate: [unverified, to verify Stage 3] Springer typical 4–9 months
candidates.ai_disclosure_stance: [unverified, to verify Stage 3] Springer Nature standard policy (see above)
candidates.oa_apc: Springer hybrid; APC ~$3060 if OA chosen
candidates.editorial_signal: [unverified, to verify Stage 3] founding editor Andrew Granville; check current board

candidates.scope_justification: Broad number-theory venue; accepts computational work but trends toward analytic and algebraic number theory rather than CF/Khinchin specifically.
candidates.typical_paper_type_match: Moderate; null results less common in IJNT historically.
candidates.decision_time_estimate: [unverified, to verify Stage 3] World Sci typical 6–12 months
candidates.ai_disclosure_stance: [unverified, to verify Stage 3] World Sci added AI disclosure clause in 2023; permissive with disclosure
candidates.editorial_signal: [unverified, to verify Stage 3] check for computational-NT editors

candidates.scope_justification: PSLQ-as-algorithm content fits; however the manuscript is mostly evidential (a null finding) rather than algorithm-development, which weakens the fit.
candidates.typical_paper_type_match: Moderate — JSC favours algorithmic/complexity contributions over null-evidence reports.
candidates.decision_time_estimate: [unverified, to verify Stage 3] Elsevier median 3–6 months for desk + 1st review
candidates.ai_disclosure_stance: [unverified, to verify Stage 3] Elsevier 2023 policy: generative-AI use must be disclosed; no co-authorship — permissive with disclosure
candidates.editorial_signal: [unverified, to verify Stage 3] Hoon Hong + symbolic-computation board; check for integer-relation editors

candidates.scope_justification: Targets computational and symbolic content; can host PSLQ + precision-control methodology papers.
candidates.typical_paper_type_match: Moderate; volume is lower than JSC; null-result tolerance unverified.
candidates.decision_time_estimate: [unverified, to verify Stage 3] Springer typical 4–9 months
candidates.ai_disclosure_stance: [unverified, to verify Stage 3] Springer Nature standard
candidates.oa_apc: Springer hybrid; APC similar to other Springer titles
candidates.editorial_signal: [unverified, to verify Stage 3] check current board

candidates.scope_justification: Broad pure/computational mathematics venue; covers experimental number-theory work; full OA model.
candidates.typical_paper_type_match: Moderate — Open Math publishes wide range; null-result tolerance moderate; APC is the main barrier for pseudonymous authors with no institutional waiver.
candidates.decision_time_estimate: [unverified, to verify Stage 3] De Gruyter median 3–6 months
candidates.ai_disclosure_stance: [unverified, to verify Stage 3] De Gruyter has explicit generative-AI disclosure policy
candidates.oa_apc: Full OA, APC ~$1000–1200 (relatively low vs Springer/Elsevier)
candidates.editorial_signal: [unverified, to verify Stage 3] large editorial board; check for number-theory editors
candidates.pseudonymity_friction: Low — ORCID accepted; APC is the friction point

candidates.scope_justification: Broad mathematics journal; accepts computational and experimental work but is not a primary destination for CF/Khinchin material.
candidates.typical_paper_type_match: Low-to-moderate; longer turnaround times reported in the literature.
candidates.decision_time_estimate: [unverified, to verify Stage 3] T&F typical 6–12 months
candidates.ai_disclosure_stance: [unverified, to verify Stage 3] T&F has 2023 AI policy — disclosure required, no co-authorship

candidates.venue: Bulletin of the Australian Mathematical Society
candidates.scope_justification: Short-paper format (under 12 pp.) fits the manuscript exactly; publishes original-research notes in number theory including computational/experimental items. Has been a frequent home for negative/null results in classical number theory.
candidates.typical_paper_type_match: Yes — short-note format with computational content is squarely in remit; null results acceptable when calibration value is clear.
candidates.decision_time_estimate: [unverified, to verify Stage 3] BAustMS typical 3–6 months first decision
candidates.ai_disclosure_stance: [unverified, to verify Stage 3] CUP / CJM-style AI disclosure policy: tools disclosed, no co-authorship
candidates.oa_apc: Hybrid OA via CUP; subscription default; APC ~$3000 if chosen
candidates.pseudonymity_friction: Low — ORCID accepted, no institutional letterhead required

candidates.scope_justification: Broad mathematics, full diamond OA (no APC). Suitable as a low-friction fallback for short experimental notes.
Always-on fallback. Khinchin-PSLQ null result is a typical math.NT preprint; Zenodo gives a persistent DOI; bypasses pseudonymity/APC frictions entirely.
candidates.typical_paper_type_match: Yes (preprint, not peer-reviewed journal). Used in this portfolio already (P-AEAL, P-ZTEK, P-CMF, etc. on Zenodo).
candidates.oa_apc: Free (arXiv) / free at standard Zenodo tier
candidates.concurrent_submission_flag: Compatible with any journal submission as a preprint per most journals' green-OA policies (to be confirmed for primary target in Stage 4).

notes_for_stage3: All [unverified] fields above MUST be checked against current venue web pages in Stage 3 before any candidate is recommended. Per the bibliographic-identifier pre-verification rule applied by analogy to venue-policy pre-verification, no policy claim should propagate to Stage 5 AEAL entries without a web-resolved URL and date.
